cases.title: SELECT_LIST: prefix 'u' expands users table + matches user_id columns

expected.comment: Table-name expansion: users.* in schema order. Column-name matching: orders.user_id, products.unit_price

cases.title: WHERE: prefix 'us' matches table name, uses table name not alias
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM users u WHERE us|

expected.comment: Alias 'u' exists but 'us' != 'u' so NOT alias-exact-match. Table-name expansion: users.* in schema order. WHERE is scope-restricted so no orders.user_id.

cases.title: JOIN ON: prefix 'u' expands users + matches user_id
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u|

expected.comment: Alias exact match: 'u' == alias 'u' -> show only u.* columns in schema order

cases.title: ORDER BY: prefix 'u' expands users table
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY u|

expected.comment: ORDER BY is scope-restricted. Only orders table in scope. Column-name matching: orders.user_id

cases.title: GROUP BY: prefix 's' matches status column
cases.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users GROUP BY s|

expected.comment: GROUP BY is scope-restricted. Only users table in scope. Column-name matching: users.status

cases.title: HAVING: prefix 'u' expands users table
cases.sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM orders GROUP BY status HAVING u|

expected.comment: HAVING is scope-restricted. Only orders table in scope. Column-name matching: orders.user_id

cases.title: Deduplication: column appears in both table expansion and column match

expected.comment: Table-name expansion: users.* in schema order. Column-name matching: orders.user_id, products.unit_price. Deduplication applied.

cases.title: Multi-query: second query context isolated from first

expected.comment: Multi-query separation: second query has no scope. Table-name expansion: users.* in schema order. Column-name matching: orders.user_id, products.unit_price

cases.title: WHERE after operator: prefix 'u' expands users table
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = u|

expected.comment: WHERE is scope-restricted. Only orders table in scope. Column-name matching: orders.user_id

cases.title: JOIN ON after operator: prefix 'o' with alias exact match
cases.sql: SELECT * FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o|

expected.comment: Alias exact match: 'o' == alias 'o' -> show only o.* columns in schema order
